name: BFS Level Order (queue)
summary: Build the tree, then sweep it level by level with a FIFO queue, emitting each level's values left to right.
visualizationIntent: init: build the binary tree from the level-order array — nodes pop in and connect to their parents; seed the queue with the root. loop: process one whole level at a time. move: dequeue the front node (current, cursor ring), append its value to the level, and enqueue its children (frontier). return: show the collected levels, one row per depth.

lineExplanations.3: Start an empty result list — one row per tree level.
lineExplanations.4: Count how many array slots the tree description has.
lineExplanations.5: If the array is empty there's no tree.
lineExplanations.6: Return the empty result — nothing to traverse.
lineExplanations.7: Start an empty list of the real tree nodes.
lineExplanations.8: Start a lookup from a node's id to the node itself.
lineExplanations.9: Start a status list (0 = unseen, 1 = in the queue, 2 = visited).
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every array slot to give it a status entry.
lineExplanations.12: child walks the array, handing out children to each real node in order.
lineExplanations.13: Loop over every array slot to build the nodes.
lineExplanations.14: Skip null slots — only real values become nodes.
lineExplanations.15: Create this node with its id, value, and empty child links.
lineExplanations.16: If there's an array slot left for a left child…
lineExplanations.18: link it as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.19: Consume that slot whether or not it was a child.
lineExplanations.20: If there's an array slot left for a right child…
lineExplanations.22: link it as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.24: Add the finished node to the node list.
lineExplanations.25: Record it in the id-to-node lookup.
lineExplanations.27: Enqueue the root (id 0) to start the sweep.
lineExplanations.29: head marks the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.30: Placeholder for the node being visited; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.31: Keep going while the queue still holds nodes.
lineExplanations.32: How many nodes are in the current level (everything queued so far).
lineExplanations.33: Start an empty row for this level's values.
lineExplanations.34: Counter for how many of this level's nodes we've processed.
lineExplanations.35: Process exactly the nodes that belong to this level.
lineExplanations.36: Look at the node at the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.38: Remember this node as the one being visited.
lineExplanations.40: Fetch the node object from the lookup.
lineExplanations.41: Append its value to the current level's row.
lineExplanations.42: If it has a left child…
lineExplanations.43: enqueue the left child for the next level,
lineExplanations.45: If it has a right child…
lineExplanations.46: enqueue the right child for the next level,
lineExplanations.48: Advance to the next node in this level.
lineExplanations.49: The level is complete — add its row to the result.
lineExplanations.50: Every level is done — return the level-order traversal.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def levelOrder(self, values): defines the method; values is the level-order array (null = missing node).
syntaxExplanations.3: [] creates an empty list for the result rows.
syntaxExplanations.5: if n == 0: tests for an empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.6: return result exits with the empty list.
syntaxExplanations.7: [] creates the list of node objects.
syntaxExplanations.8: {} creates an empty dictionary keyed by node id.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): loops i over each slot index.
syntaxExplanations.11: status.append(0) adds an 'unseen' marker for slot i.
syntaxExplanations.12: child = 1 starts the child-slot walker just after the root.
syntaxExplanations.13: for i in range(n): loops over the slots again to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.14: values[i] is not None skips the null placeholders.
syntaxExplanations.15: A dict literal storing id, value, and left/right child links (None for now).
syntaxExplanations.16: child < n checks there's a slot left to be a left child.
syntaxExplanations.17: values[child] is not None checks that slot holds a real node.
syntaxExplanations.18: node["left"] = child records the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.19: child = child + 1 moves past this slot.
syntaxExplanations.20: child < n checks there's a slot left to be a right child.
syntaxExplanations.21: values[child] is not None checks that slot holds a real node.
syntaxExplanations.22: node["right"] = child records the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.23: child = child + 1 moves past this slot.
syntaxExplanations.24: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.25: by_id[i] = node stores it under its id for fast lookup.
syntaxExplanations.29: head = 0 starts the queue's front pointer.
syntaxExplanations.30: cur_id = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid id.
syntaxExplanations.31: while head < len(queue): runs while unprocessed nodes remain.
syntaxExplanations.32: len(queue) - head is how many nodes sit in the current level.
syntaxExplanations.33: [] creates the row for this level's values.
syntaxExplanations.34: j = 0 counts nodes processed within this level.
syntaxExplanations.35: while j < level_size: processes exactly this level's nodes.
syntaxExplanations.36: queue[head] reads the front node's id.
syntaxExplanations.38: cur_id = node_id remembers the node being visited.
syntaxExplanations.42: node["left"] is not None checks for a left child.
syntaxExplanations.45: node["right"] is not None checks for a right child.
syntaxExplanations.48: j = j + 1 moves to the next node in the level.
syntaxExplanations.50: return result hands back all the levels.
